name: red-team-tools-and-methodology
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "follow red team methodology", "perform bug bounty hunting", "automate reconnaissance", "hunt for XSS vulnerabilities", "enumerate subdomains", or needs security researcher techniques and tool configurations from top bug bounty hunters.
capabilities: Guide subdomain enumeration using Amass, Subfinder, and Assetfinder
Perform live host discovery with httpx and httprobe
Execute automated vulnerability scanning with Nuclei templates
Run content discovery and directory bruteforce with ffuf
Extract historical URLs using Wayback Machine and GAU
Build automated XSS testing pipelines with Dalfox
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation for legitimate security testing tools and methodologies used in authorized bug bounty programs. The detected 'external_commands' are standard reconnaissance tools (amass, subfinder, httpx, nuclei) documented for educational purposes. The 'network' findings are example URLs and public reconnaissance services. No malicious code, exploits, or unauthorized access methods are present. The skill appropriately includes constraints for authorized testing within scope.
valueStatement: Security researchers need structured methodology to discover vulnerabilities efficiently. This skill provides proven workflows from top bug bounty hunters for subdomain enumeration, live host discovery, and vulnerability scanning.
limitations: Does not include actual exploits or attack payloads
Requires user to have proper authorization for target systems
Tool installation and API keys are user responsibility
Does not perform testing - provides methodology and command references only

useCases.targetUser: Bug bounty hunters and security researchers
useCases.title: Set Up Bug Bounty Recon Pipeline
useCases.description: Automate the initial reconnaissance phase when starting a new bug bounty program engagement.

useCases.title: Learn Professional Testing Workflows
useCases.description: Study documented methodologies from experienced security professionals to improve testing efficiency.

useCases.targetUser: Red team members and penetration testers
useCases.title: Reference Security Tool Commands
useCases.description: Quickly look up correct syntax and options for common security testing tools during engagements.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner Subdomain Discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to enumerate subdomains for target.com using passive reconnaissance tools.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new bug bounty target and need to find all subdomains

promptTemplates.title: Live Host Identification
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the workflow for discovering live web hosts from a list of subdomains?
promptTemplates.scenario: Have subdomain list and need to find which ones are actually responding

promptTemplates.title: Automated Vulnerability Scanning
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a comprehensive Nuclei scan workflow for discovered hosts.
promptTemplates.scenario: Want to run automated vulnerability scans on identified targets

promptTemplates.title: XSS Hunting Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an automated XSS testing pipeline using paramspider, Gxss, and dalfox.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to efficiently test for XSS vulnerabilities across many parameters

bestPractices: Always verify you have authorization before testing any target
Respect bug bounty program rules and scope limitations
Use rate limiting to avoid blocking or DoS
Manually verify automated tool findings before reporting
antiPatterns: Testing systems outside of authorized scope
Skipping manual verification of automated scanner results
Ignoring program rules about rate limits and testing restrictions

faq.question: Do I need special tools installed?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill documents commands for tools like Amass, Subfinder, httpx, Nuclei, and ffuf. You will need to install these tools on your system.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for penetration testing?
faq.answer: Yes, the methodology applies to both bug bounty and penetration testing engagements. Always ensure you have authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill run scans automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides methodology and command references. You must execute the commands yourself with your own tools.

faq.question: Are API keys required?
faq.answer: Some advanced features require API keys for services like Shodan, Censys, or paid data sources. The skill works without them but with limited functionality.

faq.question: What is the target audience for this skill?
faq.answer: Bug bounty hunters, penetration testers, red team members, and security researchers who want structured testing methodologies.

faq.question: Is this skill legal to use?
faq.answer: The methodology is legal when used for authorized security testing. Always obtain proper authorization through bug bounty programs or penetration testing contracts.
